title: Myrcia neorostrata
taxonomy: Myrcia neorostrata is classified within the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes over 3,000 species of plants, many of which are shrubs or small trees. Myrcia neorostrata is also classified within the tribe Myrteae, which contains many species of myrtle trees and shrubs. The genus Myrcia is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, with many species found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. Myrcia neorostrata is a specific species within this genus and is likely endemic to a specific region within its native range.

intro: Myrcia neorostrata, also known as the "Brazilian Rosemary", is a plant species in the family Myrtaceae. This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Myrcia neorostrata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
